items.title: Get Found gauge shows real non-zero Maps impressions for an active location
items.precondition: PR #211 deployed to dev AND at least one daily Proofline run has completed since that deploy. Until a post-deploy run lands, the gauge legitimately still reads 0 — that is the run not having happened, not a fix failure.
steps: 1. Log into https://dev.getalloro.com as One Endodontics / Woodbridge (org 39, location 20).
2. Open Patient Journey Insights -> Get Found (Google Visibility).
3. Read the impressions figure on the gauge.
4. Manually pull the GBP Performance API for that listing's most-recent published day and compare.
items.expected: Gauge shows a real non-zero impressions number that matches (within rounding) the API's most-recent published day. Not 0.
items.notes: This read-only review session cannot deploy or run the daily agent; operator or computer-use agent runs this after deploy. Alt proof sites: Garrison org 5, Artful org 8.

items.title: Stored row carries the day Google actually published, not calendar yesterday
items.precondition: Daily run completed post-deploy; read access to the dev DB (this session has none).
steps: 1. Read the latest google_data_store row for org 39 / location 20.
3. Read the matching agent_results proofline row's date_end.
items.expected: data_date is the real published day (typically ~3-4 days before yesterday), never calendar yesterday; impressions_maps_desktop is that day's measured value; agent_results.date_end equals the same published day.

items.title: Empty-window location reads "no recent data", never 0
items.precondition: A location whose GBP published nothing across the trailing 7-day window (or forced via a reference date), fix deployed.
steps: 1. Open Get Found (Google Visibility) for a location with no recently-published GBP data.
2. Observe the empty state on the gauge.
3. Backstop: inspect its google_data_store row — gbp_data.yesterday.data_date and the visibility object.
items.expected: Gauge shows an honest 'no recent data' / missing-metric state, NOT a 0. Stored row has data_date: null and the visibility object OMITTED, so stageReaders excludes it from day-coverage rather than counting a measured zero.
items.notes: This is the load-bearing distinction — a false 0 here is the exact bug being fixed.

items.title: A genuine measured zero stays a zero (distinct from no-data)
items.precondition: A covered day whose GBP impression entry has value omitted (a real reported zero).
steps: 1. Identify a covered day in the window whose impression value is a reported 0 (entry present, value omitted).
2. Inspect that day's stored visibility object.
items.expected: visibility object is PRESENT reading 0 (a real measured zero) — distinct from the omitted-object no-data case in T3. '0 impressions' and 'no recent data' stay different all the way to the dashboard.

items.title: Cross-metric skew cannot fabricate a zero; divergence is logged
items.precondition: First daily run after deploy; PM2/server logs accessible.
steps: 1. On the first post-deploy run, read the [DAILY] log lines for an active org.
2. Find the 'impressions newest=... interactions newest=...' note if the families diverged.
3. Cross-check the stored gbp_data.yesterday.data_date vs engagement.data_date.
items.expected: When interactions publish ahead of impressions, the impressions row resolves to impressions' OWN newest day (never the interactions-only day), engagement carries its own later data_date, and the divergence is logged rather than silently assumed.
items.notes: This is the single assumption the merged impressions-only diagnostic is blind to; the log line settles it against the live API.

items.title: Daily narrative 'new reviews' not inflated by the wider fetch
items.precondition: Daily run completed for a location with recent reviews.
steps: 1. Read the proofline agent_results.agent_input JSON for the location.
items.expected: newReviews reflects only the prior ~2-day meaning (reviewsSince = dayBeforeYesterday), not 7 days; the same review is not re-reported across the week; a review with an unreadable timestamp is kept.
items.notes: Widening the GBP fetch to 7 days silently widened the review window; this confirms the re-narrowing held.
